summary_one_line: Input must contain a well-formed 'post' table whose glyph-name string has nonzero length so find_glyph_name returns a non-empty hb_bytes_t.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A 'post' table directory entry with the 4-byte ASCII tag 70 6f 73 74 ('post') at the table-record offset, plus a valid post-table body (version 0x00020000 with a usable glyph-name index/string array) so that find_glyph_name(glyph) returns a string with s.length != 0, taking the false branch at line 165.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All 5 winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'post' tag (70 6f 73 74) at offset 0x0c-0x0f and a populated body (e.g. a run of 0x43 'C' name-string bytes at 0x20+), so find_glyph_name is invoked 4770 times for W vs 10 for L and cmp_gids runs 2370 times (0 for L) — the table is parsed and a non-empty name string is found.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds lack the 'post' magic at offset 0x0c-0x0f (W shows 70(p)x5/6f(o)x5/73(s)x5/74(t)x5 while L shows arbitrary bytes there); their s.length stays 0, so line 165 always takes the true branch (return false) and the false side stays blocked across all 10 trials.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback. The 4-byte 'post' tag comparison inside the table-directory lookup is a magic-bytes equality gate; I2SRandReplace captures the expected tag operand from the CMP log and substitutes it directly into the input, synthesizing the 'post' literal that value_profile's distance-bucket feedback alone cannot complete.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is the false side of `if (!s.length) return false;` reached only when find_glyph_name returns a non-empty string.
The single decisive pair is value_profile_cmplog beating value_profile with technique delta I2S.
Winner resolves the false branch on every trial while value_profile never does.
Winner-resolving seeds carry the literal 'post' table tag at offset 0x0c-0x0f.
The 'post' magic appears verbatim in winner seed bytes.
Loser-blocking seeds do not contain the 'post' tag at those offsets; the offsets differ across W and L.
find_glyph_name and cmp_gids run heavily for the winner and barely or never for the loser, showing the post table is parsed only for W.
value_profile_cmplog adds the I2SRandReplace mutator that value_profile lacks.
value_profile has no I2SRandReplace mutator, only havoc and token stack.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 5611 --role W --fuzzer value_profile_cmplog --trial 1 --seed a5465e0cbf5a7934`; chain has 27 levels and the tool reports 'I2S-floor signal: 1 ancestor dash row(s) at depth 26' (mutation_op = -, ParentInfo-only), the floor signature of an unwrapped I2SRandReplace find in vpc under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on a non-CMP property (e.g. an input-length threshold with no 4-byte literal equality) where value_profile_cmplog still beats value_profile would show the divergence is not driven by the 'post' magic-bytes I2S substitution.

weakest_evidence_point: I infer the full post-table body (version + name-string array yielding nonzero s.length) is required, but the byte diff only proves the 4-byte 'post' tag differs; downstream body validity is inferred from find_glyph_name/cmp_gids hit counts rather than directly diffed.
